info.title: OpenAI Sora Video API
info.description: OpenAI Sora video generation API. Generate, extend, remix, and manage AI-generated videos using models like sora-2 and sora-2-pro.

post.summary: Create a new video generation job from a prompt and optional reference assets.

request.curl:
curl https://api.openai.com/v1/videos \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $OPENAI_API_KEY" \
  -F "model=sora-2" \
  -F "prompt=A calico cat playing a piano on stage"
request.python:
import os
from openai import OpenAI

client = OpenAI(
    api_key=os.environ.get("OPENAI_API_KEY"),  # This is the default and can be omitted
)
video = client.videos.create(
    prompt="x",
)
print(video.id)
request.ruby:
require "openai"

openai = OpenAI::Client.new(api_key: "My API Key")

video = openai.videos.create(prompt: "x")

puts(video)

post.description: Create a new video generation job from a prompt and optional reference assets.

get.summary: List recently generated videos for the current project.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: Sort order of results by timestamp. Use `asc` for ascending order or `desc` for descending order.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Identifier for the last item from the previous pagination request

schema.description: Identifier for the last item from the previous pagination request

request.python:
import os
from openai import OpenAI

client = OpenAI(
    api_key=os.environ.get("OPENAI_API_KEY"),  # This is the default and can be omitted
)
page = client.videos.list()
page = page.data[0]
print(page.id)
request.ruby:
require "openai"

openai = OpenAI::Client.new(api_key: "My API Key")

page = openai.videos.list

puts(page)

get.description: List recently generated videos for the current project.

post.summary: Create a character from an uploaded video.

request.python:
import os
from openai import OpenAI

client = OpenAI(
    api_key=os.environ.get("OPENAI_API_KEY"),  # This is the default and can be omitted
)
response = client.videos.create_character(
    name="x",
    video=b"Example data",
)
print(response.id)
request.ruby:
require "openai"

openai = OpenAI::Client.new(api_key: "My API Key")

response = openai.videos.create_character(name: "x", video: StringIO.new("Example data"))

puts(response)

post.description: Create a character from an uploaded video.

parameters.name: character_id
parameters.description: The identifier of the character to retrieve.

request.python:
import os
from openai import OpenAI

client = OpenAI(
    api_key=os.environ.get("OPENAI_API_KEY"),  # This is the default and can be omitted
)
response = client.videos.get_character(
    "char_123",
)
print(response.id)
request.ruby:
require "openai"

openai = OpenAI::Client.new(api_key: "My API Key")

response = openai.videos.get_character("char_123")

puts(response)

post.summary: Create a new video generation job by editing a source video or existing generated video.

request.python:
import os
from openai import OpenAI

client = OpenAI(
    api_key=os.environ.get("OPENAI_API_KEY"),  # This is the default and can be omitted
)
video = client.videos.edit(
    prompt="x",
    video=b"Example data",
)
print(video.id)
request.ruby:
require "openai"

openai = OpenAI::Client.new(api_key: "My API Key")

video = openai.videos.edit(prompt: "x", video: StringIO.new("Example data"))

puts(video)

post.description: Create a new video generation job by editing a source video or existing generated video.

post.summary: Create an extension of a completed video.

request.python:
import os
from openai import OpenAI

client = OpenAI(
    api_key=os.environ.get("OPENAI_API_KEY"),  # This is the default and can be omitted
)
video = client.videos.extend(
    prompt="x",
    seconds="4",
    video=b"Example data",
)
print(video.id)
request.ruby:
require "openai"

openai = OpenAI::Client.new(api_key: "My API Key")

video = openai.videos.extend_(prompt: "x", seconds: :"4", video: StringIO.new("Example data"))

puts(video)

post.description: Create an extension of a completed video.

get.summary: Fetch the latest metadata for a generated video.

parameters.name: video_id
parameters.description: The identifier of the video to retrieve.

request.python:
import os
from openai import OpenAI

client = OpenAI(
    api_key=os.environ.get("OPENAI_API_KEY"),  # This is the default and can be omitted
)
video = client.videos.retrieve(
    "video_123",
)
print(video.id)
request.ruby:
require "openai"

openai = OpenAI::Client.new(api_key: "My API Key")

video = openai.videos.retrieve("video_123")

puts(video)

get.description: Fetch the latest metadata for a generated video.

delete.summary: Permanently delete a completed or failed video and its stored assets.

parameters.name: video_id
parameters.description: The identifier of the video to delete.

request.python:

client = OpenAI(

video = client.videos.delete(

request.ruby:

openai = OpenAI::Client.new(api_key: "My API Key")

delete.description: Permanently delete a completed or failed video and its stored assets.

get.summary:
Download the generated video bytes or a derived preview asset.

Streams the rendered video content for the specified video job.

parameters.name: video_id
parameters.description: The identifier of the video whose media to download.

parameters.name: variant
parameters.description: Which downloadable asset to return. Defaults to the MP4 video.

200.description: The video bytes or preview asset that matches the requested variant.

request.python:
import os
from openai import OpenAI

client = OpenAI(
    api_key=os.environ.get("OPENAI_API_KEY"),  # This is the default and can be omitted
)
response = client.videos.download_content(
    video_id="video_123",
)
print(response)
content = response.read()
print(content)
request.ruby:
require "openai"

openai = OpenAI::Client.new(api_key: "My API Key")

response = openai.videos.download_content("video_123")

puts(response)

get.description:
Download the generated video bytes or a derived preview asset.

Streams the rendered video content for the specified video job.

post.summary: Create a remix of a completed video using a refreshed prompt.

parameters.name: video_id
parameters.description: The identifier of the completed video to remix.

request.python:
import os
from openai import OpenAI

client = OpenAI(
    api_key=os.environ.get("OPENAI_API_KEY"),  # This is the default and can be omitted
)
video = client.videos.remix(
    video_id="video_123",
    prompt="x",
)
print(video.id)
request.ruby:
require "openai"

openai = OpenAI::Client.new(api_key: "My API Key")

video = openai.videos.remix("video_123", prompt: "x")

puts(video)

post.description: Create a remix of a completed video using a refreshed prompt.

video.description: Reference to the completed video to extend.

prompt.description: Updated text prompt that directs the extension generation.

seconds.description: Length of the newly generated extension segment in seconds (allowed values: 4, 8, 12, 16, 20).

CreateVideoExtendJsonBody.title: Create video extension JSON request
CreateVideoExtendJsonBody.description: JSON parameters for extending an existing generated video.

oneOf.description: Reference to the completed video to edit.

prompt.description: Text prompt that describes how to edit the source video.

CreateVideoEditMultipartBody.title: Create video edit multipart request
CreateVideoEditMultipartBody.description: Parameters for editing an existing generated video.

model.description: The video generation model to use (allowed values: sora-2, sora-2-pro). Defaults to `sora-2`.

prompt.description: Text prompt that describes the video to generate.

input_reference.description: Optional reference object that guides generation. Provide exactly one of `image_url` or `file_id`.

seconds.description: Clip duration in seconds (allowed values: 4, 8, 12). Defaults to 4 seconds.

size.description: Output resolution formatted as width x height (allowed values: 720x1280, 1280x720, 1024x1792, 1792x1024). Defaults to 720x1280.

CreateVideoJsonBody.title: Create video JSON request
CreateVideoJsonBody.description: JSON parameters for creating a new video generation job.

oneOf.description: Reference to the completed video to extend.

prompt.description: Updated text prompt that directs the extension generation.

seconds.description: Length of the newly generated extension segment in seconds (allowed values: 4, 8, 12, 16, 20).

CreateVideoExtendMultipartBody.title: Create video extension multipart request
CreateVideoExtendMultipartBody.description: Multipart parameters for extending an existing generated video.

prompt.description: Updated text prompt that directs the remix generation.

CreateVideoRemixBody.title: Create video remix request
CreateVideoRemixBody.description: Parameters for remixing an existing generated video.

object.description: The type of object returned, must be `list`.

anyOf.description: The ID of the first item in the list.
The ID of the last item in the list.

has_more.description: Whether there are more items available.

code.description: A machine-readable error code that was returned.

message.description: A human-readable description of the error that was returned.

Error-2.title: Error
Error-2.description: An error that occurred while generating the response.

anyOf.description: Identifier for the character creation cameo.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp (in seconds) when the character was created.

id.description: Unique identifier for the video job.

object.description: The object type, which is always `video`.

model.description: The video generation model that produced the job.

status.description: Current lifecycle status of the video job.

progress.description: Approximate completion percentage for the generation task.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) for when the job was created.

anyOf.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) for when the job completed, if finished.
Unix timestamp (seconds) for when the downloadable assets expire, if set.
The prompt that was used to generate the video.

size.description: The resolution of the generated video.

seconds.description: Duration of the generated clip in seconds. For extensions, this is the stitched total duration.

anyOf.description: Identifier of the source video if this video is a remix.
Error payload that explains why generation failed, if applicable.

VideoResource.title: Video job
VideoResource.description: Structured information describing a generated video job.

id.description: The identifier of the completed video.

video.description: Reference to the completed video to edit.

prompt.description: Text prompt that describes how to edit the source video.

CreateVideoEditJsonBody.title: Create video edit JSON request
CreateVideoEditJsonBody.description: JSON parameters for editing an existing generated video.

video.description: Video file used to create a character.

name.description: Display name for this API character.

CreateVideoCharacterBody.title: Create character request
CreateVideoCharacterBody.description: Parameters for creating a character from an uploaded video.

image_url.description: A fully qualified URL or base64-encoded data URL.

object.description: The object type that signals the deletion response.

deleted.description: Indicates that the video resource was deleted.

DeletedVideoResource.title: Deleted video response
DeletedVideoResource.description: Confirmation payload returned after deleting a video.

model.description: The video generation model to use (allowed values: sora-2, sora-2-pro). Defaults to `sora-2`.

prompt.description: Text prompt that describes the video to generate.

oneOf.description: Optional reference asset upload or reference object that guides generation.

seconds.description: Clip duration in seconds (allowed values: 4, 8, 12). Defaults to 4 seconds.

size.description: Output resolution formatted as width x height (allowed values: 720x1280, 1280x720, 1024x1792, 1792x1024). Defaults to 720x1280.

CreateVideoMultipartBody.title: Create video multipart request
CreateVideoMultipartBody.description: Multipart parameters for creating a new video generation job.
